_note: Curated Fringe venue dataset for Northern Belle Edinburgh Fringe day-trippers (29 Aug 2026). Lat/lng vetted from venue postcodes. Walking minutes from Edinburgh Waverley based on Google Maps walking estimates at a relaxed pace (approx. 80m / minute). edfringe.com links use the venue code in the official URL pattern so visitors land on live current-day listings for that venue.

venues.name: Pleasance Courtyard
venues.blurb: Cobbled courtyard atmosphere, where Phoebe Waller-Bridge, Fleabag and dozens of household names first broke through. Pints in the sun between shows.

venues.name: Pleasance Dome
venues.blurb: The Pleasance's second hub on Bristo Square. Sleek student-union setting next door to Underbelly and Gilded Balloon, so you can hop between four venues in one square.

venues.name: Assembly George Square
venues.blurb: Open-air garden bar, Spiegeltent, big-name comedy and theatre. The atmospheric heart of South Side Fringe.

venues.name: Assembly Hall
venues.blurb: Grand neo-gothic hall on the Mound, a five-minute stroll from the train. The closest Big Four space to Waverley, so first choice for a tight afternoon turnaround.

venues.name: Underbelly, Bristo Square
venues.blurb: Home of the upside-down purple cow. The party-end of the festival, with a buzzing beer garden between every show.

venues.name: Underbelly Cowgate
venues.blurb: Cavernous stone vaults beneath the city. Edgier theatre and breakthrough comedy, with that famous Fringe basement-bar atmosphere.

venues.name: Gilded Balloon Teviot
venues.blurb: Baronial student union turned comedy palace. Famous for late-night variety, but plenty on early enough to catch a show and still make the train home.

venues.name: Traverse Theatre
venues.blurb: Scotland's new-writing theatre. If you want the most talked-about play of the Fringe, this is where critics gather.

venues.name: Summerhall
venues.blurb: Former veterinary college, now the home of the bolder Fringe: experimental theatre, dance, art installations and a courtyard gin bar. A short cab if you're tight on time.

venues.name: Monkey Barrel Comedy
venues.blurb: Edinburgh's hottest year-round comedy club, two minutes off the Royal Mile. The locals' pick for stand-up and the closest serious comedy venue to Waverley.

venues.name: The Stand Comedy Club
venues.blurb: Beloved 165-seat comedy basement just over the North Bridge. Lineups handpicked by people who know exactly who is about to break through.

venues.name: ZOO Southside
venues.blurb: Long the dance and physical theatre stronghold of the Fringe, hidden in a converted Methodist church.

venues.name: Just the Tonic at The Caves
venues.blurb: Stone-vaulted caves a few hundred metres south of the train. Comic-spotters' favourite, full of rising names you'll boast about seeing in five years.

venues.name: Royal Mile street performers
venues.blurb: Step off the train and you're four minutes from the busking heart of the Fringe. Jugglers, magicians, sword swallowers and acoustic acts, no ticket required.
